title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: vignette demonstrates ability VEL.BMGM recover varying-coefficient functions different functional forms simultaneously, excluding noise predictors. simulate dataset five true predictors whose effects response vary nonlinearly single covariate ZZ, plus three noise predictors effect.

title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: generate n=500n = 500 observations p=8p = 8 predictors K=1K = 1 covariate Z∼Uniform(−1,1)Z \sim \text{Uniform}(-1, 1). five true coefficient functions chosen cover range functional forms: binary response generated logistic model Yi∼Bernoulli(σ(∑j=15Xijβj(Zi))).Y_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}\!\left(\sigma\!\left(\sum_{j=1}^{5} X_{ij} \beta_j(Z_i)\right)\right).

title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: fit model package defaults (squared exponential kernel, lengthscale = 0.3), provide good flexibility smooth sharp transitions. fit takes approximately 15-20 minutes modern laptop. keep vignette fast build, load precomputed results.
code: library(VEL.BMGM)  set.seed(123) n <- 500 p <- 8 K <- 1  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) Z <- matrix(runif(n, -1, 1), n, K) z <- Z[, 1]  true_fns <- list(   function(z) 2 * sin(pi * z),   function(z) 3 * z^2 - 1,   function(z) 2 * z,   function(z) -1.5 + 3 * z^2,   function(z) 2 * tanh(3 * z),   function(z) rep(0, length(z)),   function(z) rep(0, length(z)),   function(z) rep(0, length(z)) )  eta <- numeric(n) for (j in 1:5) eta <- eta + X[, j] * true_fns[[j]](z) Y <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(eta)) fit <- bmgm_GP(X, Y, Z, type = rep("c", p),                nburn = 5000, nsample = 5000, seed = 1) results_path <- system.file("extdata", "stress_test_results.RData",                              package = "VEL.BMGM") load(results_path)

title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: five true predictors selected PPI = 1.00 three noise predictors correctly excluded (PPI < 0.06). RMSE values 0.26 0.36, 95% pointwise credible intervals achieve nominal coverage.

title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: true coefficient function recovered closely posterior mean (black solid line), true function (blue dashed) lying within 95% credible ribbon entire range ZZ. model handles smooth periodic effects (sine, X1X_1), polynomial curvature (X2,X4X_2, X_4), purely linear effects (X3X_3), sharp transitions (tanh, X5X_5) without manual specification basis functions. three noise predictors (X6,X7,X8X_6, X_7, X_8) remain near zero narrow intervals, confirming spike--slab prior provides effective sparsity.
code: library(ggplot2)  df_all <- do.call(rbind, plot_data) df_all$panel <- factor(df_all$panel,                        levels = unique(df_all$panel[order(df_all$panel_order)]))  ggplot(df_all, aes(x = z)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "grey75", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_line(aes(y = est),   color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = truth), color = "blue",  linewidth = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +   facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 4, scales = "fixed") +   labs(     x = expression(Z), y = expression(beta(Z)),     caption = "Black: posterior mean | Grey: 95% credible interval | Blue dashed: truth"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"))

title: Recovery of varying coefficients
text: example uses balanced effect sizes (βj\beta_j range approximately [−2,2][-2, 2]) single predictor dominates linear predictor. effects highly imbalanced, weaker predictors can missed favor stronger ones. choice lengthscale controls smoothness assumption. Smaller values allow sharper local variation increase variance; larger values produce smoother potentially -shrunk estimates. default lengthscale = 0.3 works well across wide range varying-coefficient shapes covariates rescaled [0,1][0, 1] internally. See ?bmgm_GP full hyperparameter details.

title: Bayesian Varying-Effects Logistic Regression with Mixed Graphical Structure
text: Varying-Effects Logistic Regression & Bayesian Mixed Graphical Structure R package implements Bayesian varying-effects logistic regression binary outcomes, mixed-type predictors (continuous, discrete, zero-inflated, categorical). Predictor effects can vary nonlinearly external covariates, modeled via Gaussian Process priors. package also estimates sparse conditional independence graph among predictors, enabling structure discovery variable selection.

title: Bayesian Varying-Effects Logistic Regression with Mixed Graphical Structure
text: Flexible logistic regression: Models complex predictor effects using Gaussian Processes. Mixed data support: Handles continuous, discrete, zero-inflated, categorical predictors. Spike--slab priors: variable covariate selection, graphical model edges. Efficient MCMC: Uses Polya-Gamma augmentation fast posterior sampling. Graph estimation: Jointly estimates sparse conditional independence graph among predictors.

title: Bayesian Varying-Effects Logistic Regression with Mixed Graphical Structure
text: VEL.BMGM depends BMGM package, must installed first:
code: devtools::install_github("mauroflorez/BMGM") devtools::install_github("mauroflorez/VEL.BMGM")

title: Bayesian Varying-Effects Logistic Regression with Mixed Graphical Structure
text: Gaussian Process kernel can selected via kernel argument: "sqexp" (default) standard squared exponential kernel gives smooth posterior sample paths, "exp" produces less smooth paths can useful capturing sharper transitions. lengthscale argument controls smoothness GP; smaller values allow local variation. comprehensive demonstration multiple varying-coefficient shapes (sine, quadratic, linear, sigmoid) recovered simultaneously, see recovery varying coefficients vignette.
code: library(VEL.BMGM) set.seed(1)  n <- 300 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4)         # 4 continuous predictors Z <- matrix(runif(n, -1, 1), n, 1)      # 1 continuous covariate  # Only X[, 1] is a true predictor; its effect varies sinusoidally with Z[, 1] eta <- X[, 1] * 2 * sin(pi * Z[, 1]) Y   <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(eta))  fit <- bmgm_GP(X, Y, Z, type = rep("c", 4),                nburn = 5000, nsample = 5000, seed = 1)  # Posterior inclusion probabilities (X[, 1] should be near 1, others near 0) colMeans(fit$post_gamma[-(1:5000), ])  # Visualize the estimated varying coefficient beta_1(Z) plot(fit)

title: Bayesian Varying-Effects Logistic Regression with Mixed Graphical Structure
text: Mauro Florez package active development. Please see paper full model algorithm details.

what: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model with Varying Coefficient Logistic Regression — bmgm_GP
title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model with Varying Coefficient Logistic Regression — bmgm_GP
text: Fits Bayesian mixed graphical model logistic regression, allowing predictor effects vary covariates via Gaussian Process priors. Supports mixed-type predictors (continuous, discrete, zero-inflated, categorical) estimates sparse conditional independence graph spike--slab priors.

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model with Varying Coefficient Logistic Regression — bmgm_GP
code: bmgm_GP(   X,   Y,   Z,   type_y = "b_new",   type,   nburn = 1000,   nsample = 1000,   theta_priors,   v_0 = 0.05,   v_1 = 1,   pi_beta,   seed,   context_spec = T,   bfdr = 0.05,   cont = FALSE,   a = -2.75,   b = 0.5,   a_0 = 1,   b_0 = 1,   a_r = 1,   b_r = 100,   a_tau_w = 1,   b_tau_w = 1,   a_tau = 1,   b_tau = 1,   alpha_jk = 0.5,   lengthscale = 0.5,   sigma_kernel,   tune = 100,   kernel = "sqexp",   ... )

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model with Varying Coefficient Logistic Regression — bmgm_GP
text: X Matrix predictors (n x p). Y Binary response vector (length n). Z Matrix covariates varying effects (n x K). type_y Character; response type (default: "b_new" logistic regression). type Vector indicating type predictor X (e.g. "c" continuous, "d" discrete, "z" zero-inflated, "m" categorical). nburn Number burn-MCMC iterations. nsample Number MCMC samples retain. theta_priors List priors node (predictor). v_0, v_1 Variances spike--slab priors. pi_beta Bernoulli prior variable selection (default: 2/(p-1)). seed Optional random seed. context_spec Logical; whether use context-specific graph categorical variables. bfdr Bayesian FDR edge selection. cont Logical; apply F(X) transformation continuous variables. , b, a_0, b_0, a_r, b_r, a_tau_w, b_tau_w, a_tau, b_tau, alpha_jk, lengthscale, sigma_kernel, tune Hyperparameters priors MCMC (see details). kernel Character; kernel type GP (default: "sqexp"). ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model with Varying Coefficient Logistic Regression — bmgm_GP
text: list containing posterior samples, estimated adjacency matrices, fitted model parameters.
